User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the PG Locator application, which can be a tenant, owner, or admin.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity. This typically corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for authentication and contact.

phone.description: The phone number of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the application, determining access and permissions. (Enum: 'tenant', 'owner', 'admin')

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

Property.title: Property
Property.description: Represents a PG (Paying Guest) or Hostel property listed by an owner.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Property entity.

ownerId.description: Reference to the User who owns this property listing. (Relationship: User 1:N Property)

pgName.description: The official or marketing name of the PG/Hostel property.

city.description: The city where the property is located.

area.description: The specific area or locality within the city where the property is situated.

address.description: The full street address of the property.

rent.description: The monthly rental price for the property.

description.description: A detailed description of the property, including its features and offerings.

amenities.description: A list of amenities available at the property (e.g., 'Wi-Fi', 'AC', 'Food').

roomTypes.description: A list of room types offered within the property (e.g., 'Single Occupancy', 'Double Sharing', 'Triple Sharing').

images.description: An array of URLs pointing to images of the property, hosted on Firebase Storage.

contactNumber.description: A contact number for inquiries about this specific property.

availableBeds.description: The total number of available beds or spaces in the property.

status.description: The current moderation status of the property listing. ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the property listing was created.

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Represents a review or rating given by a tenant for a specific property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property being reviewed. (Relationship: Property 1:N Review)

userId.description: Reference to the User who submitted the review. (Relationship: User 1:N Review)

rating.description: The numerical rating given to the property, typically on a scale (e.g., 1-5).

comment.description: A textual comment or feedback provided by the user about the property.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the review was created.

definition.description: Stores user profile data, including their assigned role. Authorization relies on the path variable matching `request.auth.uid`. Also used for admin-only management of user accounts.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier (Firebase Auth UID) of the user.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for validating admin roles. A document exists here if and only if the corresponding user is an administrator. Authorization relies on existence checks and `adminId` matching `request.auth.uid` for self-management.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier (Firebase Auth UID) of an administrator.

definition.description: Stores all PG/Hostel property listings. Includes denormalized `ownerId` for authorization independence and `status` for moderation and public visibility. Supports QAPs for tenants (approved listings), owners (their own listings), and admins (all listings).

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the property listing.

definition.description: Stores all property reviews. Includes denormalized `userId` for authorization independence (owner of the review) and `propertyId` for contextual linking to the reviewed property. Supports QAPs for listing reviews by property and for users managing their own reviews.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique identifier of the review.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by adhering strictly to the core design principles. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
1.  
    `User` profiles (`/users/{userId}`): Authorization relies on the path variable `{userId}` matching `request.auth.uid`. This is inherently independent as the UID is part of the request context.
2.  
    `Property` listings (`/properties/{propertyId}`): Each `Property` document explicitly includes an `ownerId` field. This denormalizes the ownership information directly into the document. Security rules can check `resource.data.ownerId == request.auth.uid` without needing any `get()` calls to parent or related documents. This ensures atomic operations and prevents broken authorization dependencies.
3.  
    `Review` entries (`/reviews/{reviewId}`): Similar to `Property`, each `Review` document contains a `userId` field, identifying the creator of the review. Rules can verify `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` for ownership. It also contains `propertyId` for contextual linking.
4.  
    `Admin Roles` (`/roles_admin/{adminId}`): A dedicated top-level collection `roles_admin` is used for existence-based admin role checks. An admin user will have a document `adminId` matching their UID. Rules will use `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` for efficient, independent role verification, rather than relying on a `role` field in the `User` document for critical admin checks, which would involve a `get()` call.

This denormalization strategy eliminates `get()` calls in security rules, making them more performant, easier to write, and robust against race conditions in transactions.

**Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs):**
1.  
    `Public Property Search` (`/properties/{propertyId}`): Tenants can search and filter properties. The `properties` collection allows `list` operations with a `where('status', '==', 'approved')` clause. This enables efficient querying of public-facing data while ensuring only approved listings are returned, directly supporting the "Rules are not Filters" principle.
2.  
    `Owner Property Management` (`/properties/{propertyId}`): Owners can list their own properties using a query like `where('ownerId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. This allows owners to view their pending, approved, or rejected listings. The `ownerId` field in each property document facilitates this QAP.
3.  
    `Admin Moderation` (`/properties/{propertyId}` and `/reviews/{reviewId}`): Admins, verified via the `/roles_admin/{adminId}` collection, can `list` all documents in both `properties` and `reviews` collections, enabling comprehensive moderation without restrictions.

**Structural Segregation:**
*   `users` collection holds private user data, accessible by the user themselves or by administrators.
*   `roles_admin` is segregated for simple, secure admin role checks.
*   `properties` and `reviews` are top-level collections, as their primary access patterns (public search, owner's own, admin view, property-specific reviews) do not require strict hierarchical containment under a user for efficiency. The denormalized `ownerId`, `userId`, and `propertyId` fields provide the necessary links for authorization and filtering without sacrificing QAP efficiency.

This design ensures that security rules are clear, explicit, and easy to debug, directly mapping to the application's roles and features.
